title: Gate 1 baseline — pruned v4 judge on the shipped GGUF/llama.cpp stack
floor_frozen_note: Written BEFORE any Q6/Q5 ladder-tier byte exists (Plan 27-03) -- anti-goalpost-move mechanism. judge_rho below is the floor Plan 03's descent gates against; it must not be adjusted after seeing a later measurement.
stack: f16 GGUF (--no-mtp, no MTP/speculative-decoding head -- see conversion_receipt_v4.json mtp_deviation_note), ~/llama.cpp/build/bin/llama-server, -ngl 999 --parallel 4 --jinja, CTX 20480
llamacpp_build: version: 1 (8f114a9) built with GNU 13.3.0 for Linux aarch64
seeds: single-seed s1 (the capture is one serve pass, not a 3-seed ensemble)
n_dataset_note: data/reasoning_dataset/openai_val.jsonl has 141 total lines; scripts.sieve_capture_judge_http filters to rows whose first user message starts with '<wp_judge>' (its documented index discipline -- eval_relabel.py/g1_read.py enumerate identically). The other 20 lines are <wp_gen>/other examples -- irrelevant to a judge-only ship (gen retired as a deliverable 2026-07-15). n=121 is the FULL wp_judge subset, not a truncation or a skipped-example artifact. Confirmed by direct count: wp_judge=121, other=20, 121+20=141.

non_comparable_references.note: from output/prune-v4/selection_v4.json -- bf16-vLLM, a DIFFERENT STACK and (for the ensemble) a different seed configuration. Recorded for orientation only. NOT the gate floor and NOT comparable to any number in this file.

rejected_floors.reason: v1.3/v3 numbers on a different base model and an unpruned checkpoint; the codegen benchmark axis does not apply at all (judge-only ship, gen retired 2026-07-15). Recorded as explicitly REJECTED so a later reader cannot mistake their absence for an oversight.

f16_anchor_result: SUCCEEDED -- no OOM. The f16 (~57 GiB, --no-mtp) served cleanly on the 121 GiB host (114 GiB at rest); A3's documented OOM-history fallback branch was NOT triggered. f16_anchor_failure_evidence intentionally omitted -- there is no failure to record.
